page: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family. It is powered by an ARM-based Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor utilizing the ARMv8 architecture and operating at 3.0 GHz. The instance features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This server does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, relying on remote storage volumes.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average to weak performance profiles. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance reached 1822.69 and multi-core reached 7252.95, both placing in the average tier. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, with read speeds of 46.07 GB/sec and write speeds of 29.04 GB/sec, though small-block cached reads are weak at 282.3 GB/sec. Geekbench scores are generally weak, including a single-core score of 1109.0 and a multi-core score of 3672.0. Cryptographic performance is poor, as shown by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 647.61. Large language model inference speeds on a small 135M model are also poor, yielding 165.69 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 40.15 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the B4ps_v2 offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development and testing due to its burstable CPU allocation model. The primary tradeoff is the burstable CPU credit system, which makes it unsuitable for sustained high-utilization workloads. Additionally, the lack of local storage and weak cryptographic and memory performance limit its utility for heavy database operations or real-time data processing. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, software compilation testing, and general-purpose bursty workloads that do not require continuous maximum CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, featuring an ARM-based Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 4 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.0 GHz, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows average CPU performance in stress-ng tests, alongside weak memory bandwidth and poor cryptographic execution. The burstable CPU allocation model provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable CPU demands, though it introduces performance tradeoffs during sustained utilization. This server is best suited for light web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose development tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, designed for cost-efficient, variable-demand workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, suited for light, variable-demand workloads.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine offering balanced memory and compute resources for cost-conscious, variable-demand workloads.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture powered by a 4-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient handling of variable workloads
No local storage or GPU, with support for remote Premium Storage
Best suited for light web serving, software compilation, and development environments
